codeQuality.notes: Implementation is clean and well-documented. SkipLink component, useFocusManagement hook, and ARIA-enhanced components follow existing project patterns. CSS focus styles use :focus-visible consistently across buttons and nav, CSS comments are clear. The worker added descriptive aria-labels that match existing UI text semantics. All SVG decorative icons properly marked with aria-hidden. The only minor inconsistencies: SkipLink.css uses :focus instead of :focus-visible for the skip link reveal, which differs from the project's pattern of using :focus-visible for focus-related visibility.

testCoverage.notes: 27 new tests added across Accessibility.test.tsx and SkipLink.test.tsx covering skip links, navigation labels, chat message ARIA attributes, loading spinner roles, toast notifications, legal disclaimer landmarks, session summary accessibility, and layout structure. Tests properly use Testing Library role queries (getByRole, getAllByRole). Gap: no direct unit tests for the useFocusManagement hook behavior — the hook's route-change focus behavior is only indirectly tested via the Layout test. Also, the 'messages container has role=log' test creates a DOM element manually instead of rendering the actual MeditationChat component, meaning it doesn't verify the actual component output.

integrationQuality.notes: Good integration with existing codebase. Layout.tsx cleanly integrates SkipLink and useFocusManagement. ARIA enhancements across components (ErrorBoundary, ConfirmationModal, ChatMessage, Profile, Settings) all follow consistent patterns without altering core component logic. CSS additions to index.css and Navigation.css align with existing design system (using CSS variables like --color-gold-primary and --transition-fast). All 25 test files pass (242 tests). Lint and typecheck clean.

issues.description: src/components/SkipLink.css:15 — CSS uses .skip-link:focus instead of .skip-link:focus-visible. When a user clicks the skip link with a mouse (e.g., screen reader user who also uses mouse), the focus state persists showing the hidden element. All other focus states in the project use :focus-visible (buttons, nav links, toggle). Should be :focus-visible for consistency.

src/lib/useFocusManagement.ts — The hook adds blur event listeners with { once: true } but does not use useEffect cleanup to remove them if the component unmounts before blur fires. While { once: true } ensures the callback runs at most once, orphaned listeners on detached DOM elements could be a minor memory concern. Consider using an AbortController or cleanup function.

src/components/Accessibility.test.tsx:123-135 — The 'messages container has role=log' test manually creates a DOM element and sets attributes instead of rendering the actual MeditationChat component. This means the test doesn't verify that MeditationChat actually outputs the correct ARIA attributes. Consider testing the rendered MeditationChat component directly.

No unit tests for useFocusManagement hook. The hook's behavior of focusing headings on route change and cleaning up tabindex is critical for accessibility but has no dedicated test coverage. The hook is only indirectly tested via the Layout accessibility tests.

sharedStateObservations.observation: useFocusManagement hooks into #main-content via document.querySelector, creating implicit coupling between Layout.tsx and the hook. If the main-content ID changes, the hook silently fails.
sharedStateObservations.description: The hook at src/lib/useFocusManagement.ts queries document.querySelector('#main-content') directly, creating a tight coupling to Layout.tsx's main element ID. The Layout tests don't mock this behavior. This pattern isn't documented as a convention or warning in AGENTS.md. Could be a point of confusion for future workers who change the Layout structure.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Navigation.css includes focus states for .nav-list a, .nav-list button, .nav__toggle, and .nav__brand, but there's no documented convention for :focus-visible across all interactive elements.
sharedStateObservations.description: The worker added :focus-visible styles to navigation elements (src/components/Navigation.css:78-95) and button focus styles to src/styles/index.css:467-471. This is correct and consistent, but AGENTS.md doesn't document that :focus-visible (not :focus) is the project's standard for visible focus states. Future workers might accidentally use :focus, which could cause the FocusInvisible accessibility anti-pattern.

sharedStateObservations.observation: SkipLink CSS uses CSS variables (var(--color-gold-primary), var(--color-text-inverse), var(--radius-md), var(--transition-fast)) that are already used throughout the project. Good adherence to design system.
sharedStateObservations.description: The worker didn't use any undocumented CSS conventions. All color and spacing variables exist in the design system. No action needed — this is a positive observation.

summary: The polish-accessibility feature delivers comprehensive accessibility improvements that match the feature description. SkipLink component, focus management hook, enhanced ARIA labels, visible focus states, and live regions are all implemented and tested. All 242 tests pass with clean lint and typecheck results. The implementation quality is good with minor issues: SkipLink uses :focus instead of :focus-visible (inconsistency), useFocusManagement hook could have better cleanup, and the chat container role=log test should verify the actual component output rather than a manually constructed element. No blocking issues found. The feature adequately fulfills VAL-UI-007 (keyboard navigation) and VAL-UI-008 (screen reader support) requirements.
